sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Mr. Nelson, is a 72-year-old male presenting for ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis.
He reports his knee is still swollen and painful, with a pain level of approximately 3 out of 10 on a normal day, but up to 6 out of 10 at times.
The swelling has been present for about 2 months, and he has tried using compression socks, which provide some relief but are not fully effective.
He has difficulty getting up from sitting, requiring a cane for support, and experiences stiffness in his knee.
He takes Xeljanz 11 mg daily and Celebrex 200 mg daily, which he reports has been effective until the swelling recurred.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis, for which he has been receiving treatment.
He has also been taking Xeljanz and Celebrex as mentioned above.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
Xeljanz 11 mg daily
Celebrex 200 mg daily
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's knee is swollen, with pitting around the knee and ankle, but normal capillary refill in the toes.
The knee is hot to the touch, indicating inflammation.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has ongoing rheumatoid arthritis with worsening knee swelling and pain.
Problem list: Rheumatoid Arthritis; Swelling of the knee; Pain; Inflammation of the knee.
Plan:
I prescribed a steroid injection of 80 mg of triamcinolone and 1 ml of lidocaine for the knee inflammation.
I also ordered an x-ray of the knee to investigate the cause of the swelling.
I recommended the patient try reducing their salt intake and propping their feet up at night to alleviate swelling.
I advised the patient to follow up with their primary care physician to discuss further management of their condition.
I also instructed the patient to schedule blood work for CPM levels, as Xeljanz is a high-risk medication.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with his primary care physician and return for a follow-up appointment as needed.
